tasks.title: SPIKE: gitnexus MCP child tool surface investigation
tasks.notes: gitnexus 1.6.7, 13 child tools listed (docs/code-intelligence-child-surface.md). (a) Communities: NO direct tool -> rung 2 compose via cypher over Community/MEMBER_OF (214 communities confirmed). (b) Flows/processes: NO direct tool; query has processes[] but NL-only (not from/to/name filterable) -> rung 2 compose via cypher over Process/STEP_IN_PROCESS (300 processes). (c) Upstream depth-2 callers: DIRECT via impact{direction:upstream,maxDepth:2,includeTests:true} -> rung 1/2, filter byDepth filePaths with isTestPath. SURPRISE: GitNexusProvider.graph() calls non-existent child tool 'call_graph' (Unknown tool error) -> code_graph broken on 1.6.7; backlog retarget to context/cypher. cypher returns markdown table not JSON rows.

tasks.title: SPIKE: embeddings population investigation
tasks.notes: LOCAL: 'npx gitnexus analyze --embeddings' populates embeddings via a local ONNX CPU model (Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-xs, 384-dim, dist/mcp/core/embedder.js MODEL_ID); no API key. Verified end-to-end on a throwaway repo: embeddings 0 -> 3, vectorSearch flipped unavailable -> exact-scan active. Cost: one-time ~87MB HuggingFace model download (cached at ~/.cache/huggingface, offline after), then local CPU compute. Platform caveat: LadybugDB VECTOR index unavailable on win32 so semantic query uses exact-scan fallback (cap 10000 chunks) -- works, not a blocker. Embeddings are OFF by default and PRESERVED across plain analyze (only --drop-embeddings removes them). Optional EXTERNAL remote path exists via GITNEXUS_EMBEDDING_URL/_MODEL/_API_KEY env vars (opt-in, not required). T2.3 -> LOCAL branch: pass --embeddings in /pm index + VERIFY re-index. See docs/code-intelligence-embeddings.md.

tasks.title: P4b: kb_session_prime graph-neighbor expansion
tasks.notes: Expansion lives in src/tools/kb-session-prime.ts (design D4, one layer up -- SqliteProvider.prime/HttpKbProvider.prime untouched). After prime + global append, sweeps hint_symbols through getProvider().context({name}) (per T1.1 surface doc: incoming.calls[].name + outgoing.calls[].name; code_graph is broken on gitnexus 1.6.7 so context was chosen), collects neighbors not in hint_symbols capped at NEIGHBOR_CAP=10 (exported), runs ONE providers.project.query batch, appends deduped entries below direct hits marked via='graph-neighbor' capped at ADDED_ENTRY_CAP=5 (exported). Defensive: parseContextNeighbors treats isError/missing content/unparseable JSON/ambiguous-candidate responses as no neighbors; per-symbol try/catch; per-neighbor ftsSafeTerm quoted-token sanitizer so one FTS-hostile name degrades to skipping that neighbor (plan-review correctness note) not the batch; entire block hard-skip try/catch -> prime output unchanged on any failure; skipped when hint_symbols empty/absent. Tests: extended tests/knowledge/kb-session-prime.test.ts with 9 wrapper tests (vi.resetModules + dynamic import + vi.hoisted mocks per KB constraint 1) covering append+via+rank-below-direct, NEIGHBOR_CAP (11->10), ADDED_ENTRY_CAP (8->5), dedupe-by-id, graceful-skip (getProvider throws / context throws), hint_symbols absent, isError, FTS-hostile-neighbor-skipped. 13/13 pass, build clean. NOTE: pre-commit ASCII hook false-positives on backtick-n template literals -- used string concatenation in tests.

tasks.title: VERIFY Phase 1
tasks.notes: VERIFY green. (1) npm run build clean (tsc exit 0). (2) npm test: 1667 passed, 2 failed, 14 skipped -- the 2 failures are ONLY the known pre-existing timezone tests in tests/time-utils.test.ts (toLocalISOString, beads yashr-302); all 116 files otherwise pass incl. 13/13 kb-session-prime. (3) npx gitnexus analyze succeeded (exit 0, 20.5s incremental: 4101 nodes, 11045 edges, 214 clusters, 300 flows). (4) git checkout -- AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md ran; git status shows NO AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md modifications (only untracked .claude/skills/ and .claude/worktrees/, unrelated). (5) Branch feat/code-intelligence-abstraction pushed. No PR.

tasks.title: code_map tool (communities)
tasks.notes:
Gated by T1.1 Decisions table (communities row): no direct communities/map tool on gitnexus 1.6.7 -> rung 2 compose over cypher. Added codeMapSchema {repo?, top?} in src/tools/code-intelligence.ts and map(params) to CodeIntelligenceProvider + GitNexusProvider (src/tools/code-intelligence-gitnexus.ts), routing via callGitNexus('cypher', {query: MATCH (c:Community) RETURN c.heuristicLabel AS label, c.symbolCount AS symbols, c.cohesion AS cohesion, c.keywords AS keywords ORDER BY symbols DESC LIMIT $top, params: {top}, repo}). The cypher tool returns {markdown, row_count} as JSON-with-a-hint-suffix text (JSON.stringify(...) + 

---
**Next:**...); wrote extractCypherPayload() to strip the hint and parse it, plus a small exported pure parseMarkdownTable(markdown) row parser (reused by T2.2) and exported asciiSanitizeLabel() (unicode arrows/other non-ASCII -> '->'/'?', since community labels/keywords can carry non-ASCII per T1.1 spike). Registered code_map in src/index.ts next to the code_* block with routing-guidance description; does NOT parse ladybugdb directly. Tests added to tests/code-intelligence.test.ts: codeMapSchema validation (4), parseMarkdownTable pure-function unit tests (4), asciiSanitizeLabel unit tests (3), GitNexusProvider.map() mocked-cypher-response mapping incl. default/explicit top and ASCII sanitization (3), missing-index error-path reuse (1). Build clean, all tests green (34/34 in this file at commit time).

tasks.title: code_flow tool (process flows)
tasks.notes: Gated by T1.1 Decisions table (flows row): no direct flows/processes tool and code_query's processes[] is free-text-only (not from/to/name filterable) -> rung 2 compose over cypher. Added codeFlowSchema {from?, to?, name?, repo?} and flow(params) to CodeIntelligenceProvider + GitNexusProvider. flow() builds a WHERE clause on Process.heuristicLabel (name/from/to each via CONTAINS $param, ANDed together when multiple given, omitted entirely when none given) for a list query (RETURN label/processType/stepCount ORDER BY stepCount DESC LIMIT 20), then issues one follow-up cypher query per matched process (capped at MAX_FLOW_STEP_LOOKUPS=5 to bound fan-out) over MATCH (s)-[r:CodeRelation {type:'STEP_IN_PROCESS'}]->(p:Process) WHERE p.heuristicLabel = $label RETURN s.name AS step, s.filePath AS filePath, r.step AS stepOrder ORDER BY r.step -- the step-lookup query is keyed on the RAW (pre-sanitize) label so it matches the child's stored value; only the label in the final output is ASCII-sanitized. Reused T2.1's parseMarkdownTable/asciiSanitizeLabel/extractCypherPayload/isErrorResult helpers verbatim. Registered code_flow in src/index.ts with routing-guidance description. Tests added: codeFlowSchema validation (2), GitNexusProvider.flow() mocked mapping incl. name/from/to filter-to-WHERE-clause behavior (3), steps fetch + unicode-arrow sanitization (1), step-lookup cap at 5 (1), missing-index error-path reuse (1). Build clean, all tests green (42/42 in this file at commit time, split cleanly from T2.1's 34 via two separate commits).

tasks.title: Embeddings outcome implementation
tasks.notes: Gated by T1.2 spike (docs/code-intelligence-embeddings.md line 1 classification): LOCAL -- bundled ONNX CPU model (Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-xs), no API key, one-time ~87MB HuggingFace download then offline. Per the LOCAL decision branch this is docs + flag plumbing only, no config field, no code change: confirmed no src/ code path builds the 'gitnexus analyze' command line (it is only ever invoked by PM-dispatched agents via execute_command per skills/pm/index.md; the fleet's own gitnexus MCP child is a separate 'npx -y gitnexus mcp' process unaffected by this flag). Wired --embeddings into: (1) skills/pm/index.md step 3 (npx gitnexus analyze -> npx gitnexus analyze --embeddings) plus a new 'The --embeddings flag' section documenting the local-ONNX classification, the one-time ~87MB download, OFF-by-default/preserved-unless-drop-embeddings behavior, and the win32 exact-scan caveat; (2) skills/pm/doer-reviewer-loop.md's doer dispatch template VERIFY step (the literal command every PM-dispatched doer's checkpoint runs). Updated docs/code-intelligence-embeddings.md with a 'T2.3 wiring done' section recording exactly what changed. No tests (no code path touched, per plan's 'Tests: only if code is written'). Build clean (tsc exit 0, unaffected by doc-only change).

tasks.title: VERIFY Phase 2
tasks.notes: VERIFY green. (1) npm run build clean (tsc exit 0). (2) npm test: 1690 passed, 2 failed, 14 skipped -- the 2 failures are ONLY the known pre-existing timezone tests in tests/time-utils.test.ts (toLocalISOString, beads yashr-302); all 116 files otherwise pass, incl. the 42 code-intelligence.test.ts tests covering T2.1 code_map and T2.2 code_flow. (3) npx gitnexus analyze --embeddings succeeded (exit 0, 103.8s incremental: 4127 nodes, 11113 edges, 217 clusters, 300 flows; logged the expected win32 VECTOR-unavailable/exact-scan-fallback notice from docs/code-intelligence-embeddings.md -- not an error). (4) git checkout -- AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md ran; git status confirmed AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md were reverted to clean (only untracked .claude/skills/ and .claude/worktrees/ remained, unrelated). (5) Branch feat/code-intelligence-abstraction pushed. No PR. T3.1 NOT started per scope.

tasks.title: Auto-reindex module: state, decision fn, spawn
tasks.notes: New src/tools/code-intelligence-reindex.ts (design D3), no import from code-intelligence.ts (circular-import rule, mirrors freshness-module precedent confirmed by KB entry e86910b8). Module-level Map<repoPath, {runningChild?, lastFinishedAt?}>; pure exported shouldStartReindex(entry, now, cooldownMs=120000) -- single-flight (entry.running) + cooldown (now-lastFinishedAt<cooldownMs) -> false, undefined entry -> true. Config override read synchronously (readFileSync, not fs/promises) from ~/.apra-fleet/data/code-intelligence/config.json {autoReindex:{cooldownMs?,enabled?}} default enabled:true -- sync so maybeScheduleReindex's boolean return can drive T3.2's suffix without awaiting. maybeScheduleReindex(repoPath):boolean spawns npx gitnexus analyze (spawn('npx',['gitnexus','analyze'],{cwd:repoPath,detached:true,stdio:['ignore','ignore','pipe'],shell:process.platform==='win32'})), never awaited, captures a 4000-char stderr tail logged via logWarn on non-zero exit, updates lastFinishedAt+clears runningChild on child 'exit', logs via logError on spawn-throw/child 'error'/outer-catch -- entire function wrapped in try/catch, never throws. Tests (tests/code-intelligence-reindex.test.ts, 11/11 green): shouldStartReindex pure unit (running/cooldown/past-cooldown/undefined/custom-cooldownMs, 5 cases), spawn-args correctness incl. platform-dependent shell flag, single-flight (2 calls -> 1 spawn), enabled:false no-op, custom cooldownMs from config blocks a second call after the first finishes, non-zero-exit stderr-tail logging, spawn-throws never propagates. Module-singleton tests used vi.resetModules()+dynamic import per test with vi.hoisted mocks (KB constraint 1) per plan. Build clean (tsc exit 0).

tasks.title: Wire auto-reindex into freshness path + suffix
tasks.notes: Trigger point per design D3 ('already per-call, already knows repo + divergence; no new watchers, no cron'): computeFreshnessNote() in src/tools/code-intelligence-gitnexus.ts first checks divergence via freshnessNote(meta.lastCommit, head) !== null, and only when diverged calls maybeScheduleReindex(repo) (value import from ./code-intelligence-reindex.js -- no cycle, per KB circular-import entry e86910b8/16e5b35a which confirmed the freshness-module precedent this mirrors). maybeScheduleReindex is synchronous+non-blocking (spawns detached/unref'd child) so no await/latency added; wrapped in try/catch with logError('code-intelligence-gitnexus', ...) on throw so a scheduling failure never affects the tool result. freshnessNote() (code-intelligence-freshness.ts) gained a reindexScheduled:boolean=false param, stays pure (no IO), appends exact suffix ' A background re-index has been started.' (leading space) only when true. Tests: tests/code-intelligence-freshness.test.ts +4 (suffix on/off/default/still-null-when-shas-match, exact-string); tests/code-intelligence.test.ts +4 in the existing 'GitNexusProvider freshness note wiring (F2.2)' describe (divergence+decline->no suffix+called-once-with-repo, divergence+schedule-true->suffix, schedule-throws->result unaffected+no suffix+logError called, no-divergence->maybeScheduleReindex never called) via a new vi.mock('../src/tools/code-intelligence-reindex.js') + vi.mock('../src/utils/log-helpers.js') hoisted at top of file (KB constraint 1 pattern already used in this file). 67/67 green across the 3 affected test files. Build clean (tsc exit 0). No non-ASCII introduced (pre-existing unicode-arrow regex/test fixture in this file predates T3.2, verified byte-scanned).

tasks.title: P4a: code_context inlines KB entries
tasks.notes: Design D4 layering (binding): new src/tools/code-intelligence-kb-enrich.ts imported ONLY by the code_context handler in src/index.ts -- code-intelligence-gitnexus.ts (the provider file) does not import the KB service, avoiding a src/tools <-> src/services cycle. Handler now does provider.context(input) -> enrichContextWithKb(input.name, result) -> JSON.stringify(merged). enrichContextWithKb(name, result): passes through error results untouched (isError check first, no KB query at all); otherwise getKbProviders().project.query({query:name, l1_only:true, include_stale:false}), filters to entries where confidence==='CONFIRMED' && symbols.includes(name) (exact match per plan), appends a text content block '[knowledge-bank] N confirmed entries for <name>:' + one '- <title> -- <summary...120>' line per match via the same appendTextBlock content-array-preserving pattern as appendFreshnessNote in gitnexus.ts; zero matches -> result returned unchanged (no block); whole body wrapped in try/catch -> any KB error (getKbProviders throws, query throws) also returns the result unchanged, never fails the call. Tests (tests/code-intelligence-kb-enrich.test.ts, 7/7 green, vi.resetModules+dynamic import+vi.hoisted per KB constraint 1): append path with N=2 + 120-char truncation verified against the untruncated string; no-append x3 (zero results, symbols not containing name, only non-CONFIRMED matches); error path x2 (getKbProviders rejects, project.query rejects) both returning the original object unchanged; error-result pass-through verified to skip the KB query entirely (mockGetKbProviders not called). Full regression pass across code-intelligence + knowledge test dirs: 191/191 green. Build clean (tsc exit 0). ASCII-scanned clean (pre-existing non-ASCII em-dash in an unrelated src/index.ts comment and pre-existing unicode-arrow fixtures in code-intelligence.test.ts predate this change, confirmed by byte offset).

tasks.notes: VERIFY green. (1) npm run build clean (tsc exit 0). (2) npm test: 1715 passed, 2 failed, 14 skipped, 118 files (116 passed/1 failed) -- the 2 failures are ONLY the known pre-existing timezone tests in tests/time-utils.test.ts (toLocalISOString, beads yashr-302); this includes the new/extended T3.1-T3.3 suites (code-intelligence-reindex.test.ts 11, code-intelligence-kb-enrich.test.ts 7, code-intelligence-freshness.test.ts 11, code-intelligence.test.ts 45 -- all green). (3) npx gitnexus analyze --embeddings succeeded (exit 0, 25.4s incremental: 4169 nodes, 11187 edges, 222 clusters, 300 flows; logged the expected win32 VECTOR-unavailable/exact-scan-fallback notice per docs/code-intelligence-embeddings.md -- not an error). (4) git checkout -- AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md ran; git status confirmed AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md reverted to clean (only untracked .claude/skills/ and .claude/worktrees/ remained, unrelated to this sprint). (5) Branch feat/code-intelligence-abstraction pushed to origin (8400600..892e887). No PR. T4.1 NOT started per scope. KB circular-import entry (e86910b8/16e5b35a, retrieved via kb_session_prime hint_symbols + kb_query) directly informed T3.1's file-separation choice and T3.2's value-import wiring -- confirmed useful.

tasks.title: Telemetry recorder: append, rotation, handler wiring
tasks.notes: New src/tools/code-intelligence-telemetry.ts (design D8), imported ONLY by the shared handler layer in src/index.ts -- code-intelligence-gitnexus.ts untouched. recordUsage(tool, target, repo): fire-and-forget writeUsageLine() (mkdir parent -> rotateIfNeeded (fs.stat; if size>5MB fs.rename usage.jsonl->usage.jsonl.1, overwrite semantics via Node's rename replace-existing behavior on both POSIX/Windows) -> fs.appendFile one JSON line {ts:ISO8601, tool, target, repo} + newline); every step wrapped so a rejected promise or sync throw is swallowed via void writeUsageLine().catch(()=>{}) plus an outer try/catch -- never surfaces to caller, never returns a promise itself. Wired into src/index.ts's shared handler layer for the 6 existing code_* tools (code_graph:input.symbol, code_impact:input.target, code_query:input.query, code_context:input.name, code_map:'' (no natural single-string arg for a listing tool), code_flow:input.name??input.from??input.to??''), each a one-line recordUsage(toolName, <arg>, input.repo ?? null) call before the provider call. code_tests wiring deferred to T4.4 (didn't exist yet at T4.1 commit time, per plan's 'if they were built' clause). Tests (tests/code-intelligence-telemetry.test.ts, 11/11 green): append format (exact 4 keys, ISO ts round-trips, repo null when absent, repo string when given) via vi.waitFor() polling (recordUsage is sync/fire-and-forget so tests poll for the mock call rather than awaiting it); mkdir-before-append; no-rotate under threshold; rotate + overwrite rename call + fresh append when >5MB; boundary case (exactly 5MB does NOT rotate, only strictly greater); error isolation x4 (mkdir rejects, stat rejects non-ENOENT, rename rejects, appendFile rejects -- recordUsage itself never throws in any case). No module-level state (every call re-reads/re-writes fs), so KB constraint 1 (vi.resetModules+dynamic import) does not apply -- static imports + vi.mock('fs/promises') sufficient. Build clean (tsc exit 0).

tasks.title: fleet_status: top symbols (30d)
tasks.notes: Extended src/tools/check-status.ts following the codeIntelligenceHealth/codeIntelligenceCompactLine degraded-safe pattern exactly (every IO try/caught, never throws, omitted on failure). computeTopSymbols(now, usagePath, rotatedUsagePath) -- optional path params default to the real USAGE_LOG_PATH/ROTATED_USAGE_LOG_PATH exported by T4.1's code-intelligence-telemetry.ts (reused, not duplicated) -- does one pass over both files, skips unparseable lines and lines missing ts/target, filters ts>=now-30d (boundary-inclusive), aggregates count by target via a Map, returns top 5 by count descending, or undefined when no usage data/every line unparseable-or-out-of-window/read error (directory-instead-of-file, etc). Wired into fleetStatus(): after the existing codeIntelligenceHealth try/catch, a second independent try/catch calls computeTopSymbols() and sets codeIntelligence.topSymbols only if defined -- so a telemetry-read failure can never affect the rest of fleet_status, and topSymbols is populated independently of index presence (recordUsage fires on every code_* call regardless of whether an index exists). CodeIntelligenceHealth interface gained an optional topSymbols?:TopSymbol[] field, which flows into the JSON payload's existing codeIntelligence key automatically (no separate wiring needed). codeIntelligenceCompactLine() gained a topSymbolsFragment() appended to BOTH the present and no-index branches (' | top symbols (30d): a (12), b (9)', omitted entirely when topSymbols is absent/empty). Tests added to tests/fleet-status-code-intelligence.test.ts (22/22 green in this file): 3 compact-line fragment tests (present, absent, no-index-line-still-gets-it); computeTopSymbols() suite -- no-file->undefined, top-5-by-count with ties beyond the cap, <5 distinct targets, 30d exclusion, exact-30d-boundary-inclusion, reads .1 in addition to primary, unparseable-lines-skipped, missing-ts/target-lines-skipped, all-lines-unparseable-or-stale->undefined, directory-instead-of-file->undefined-not-throw. Real temp files via mkdtempSync/writeFileSync (matching this test file's existing no-fs-mocking style) rather than mocking fs, made possible by the path-override params. Build clean (tsc exit 0).

tasks.title: isTestPath pure function
tasks.notes: New src/tools/code-intelligence-tests.ts, no dependencies (pure, dependency-free per design D9 -- exported isTestPath(path):boolean). True when any path segment (split on both '/' and '\\') is exactly 'test'/'tests'/'spec' case-insensitive, OR the filename matches /\.(test|spec)\.[^.]+$/i. Tests (tests/code-intelligence-tests.test.ts, 22/22 green, table-driven): 11 positive cases (segment matches incl. mixed case and backslash separators, filename .test./.spec. matches incl. nested/backslash paths, both-match), 8 negative cases (contest/attest.ts/testfile.ts/protest.spec-with-no-trailing-extension confirming the regex requires a non-empty extension after .test/.spec, plain product paths, attestation/specimen directories which contain but are not equal to test/spec, empty string), plus 3 extra targeted cases (mixed-separator segment match, mixed-separator filename match, 'testHelpers.ts' filename substring correctly NOT matched since it's neither an exact segment nor a .test./.spec. suffix). No IO, build clean (tsc exit 0). Consumed by T4.4's code_tests tool (isTestPath imported into code-intelligence-gitnexus.ts).
